---
title: "About the Author"
---

<div class="author-profile">
  <div class="author-avatar">AF</div>
  <div class="author-meta">
    <h2 class="author-name">Anthony Faustine</h2>
    <p class="author-title">Principal ML Engineer &nbsp;·&nbsp; Analog Devices, Ireland</p>
    <div class="author-social-row">
      <a href="https://sambaiga.github.io" title="Website"><i class="bi bi-globe2"></i> sambaiga.github.io</a>
      <a href="https://github.com/sambaiga" title="GitHub"><i class="bi bi-github"></i> sambaiga</a>
      <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/sambaiga" title="LinkedIn"><i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i> sambaiga</a>
      <a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-7838-533X" title="ORCID"><i class="bi bi-award"></i> 0000-0002-7838-533X</a>
      <a href="mailto:sambaiga@gmail.com" title="Email"><i class="bi bi-envelope-at"></i> sambaiga@gmail.com</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

I am a machine learning engineer and researcher based in Ireland. My work centres on designing and deploying ML systems at scale — from probabilistic forecasting and energy disaggregation to supply chain optimisation and intelligent edge computing. Over more than ten years across academia and industry, I have worked on the full arc of a machine learning project: framing the problem, building the model, engineering the pipeline, and keeping it honest in production.

At [Analog Devices](https://www.analog.com), I architect composable ML systems for supply chain optimisation and edge inference, working across energy, industrial, and semiconductor domains. Before that, my research focused on non-intrusive load monitoring, uncertainty quantification, and probabilistic forecasting for low-voltage distribution networks, with work published in IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid, IEEE Transactions on Power Systems, and IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics.

This book grew out of a pattern I kept seeing in both research groups and engineering teams: people who were technically capable but were missing one of three things — rigorous engineering practice, genuine understanding of what the model was doing, or a clear sense of what value the work was meant to create. *From Data to Impact* is my attempt to address all three in a single, structured path.

## Open Source

<div class="project-list">

<div class="project-item">
  <div class="project-header">
    <span class="project-name">Twiga</span>
    <span class="project-year">2024</span>
  </div>
  <p class="project-desc">Production forecasting library with REST serving, MLOps integration, and hyperparameter optimisation. Used as a reference implementation for the MLOps chapters.</p>
  <div class="project-links">
    <a href="https://github.com/sambaiga/twiga"><i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub</a>
    <a href="https://pypi.org/project/twiga/"><i class="bi bi-box"></i> PyPI</a>
    <a href="https://sambaiga.github.io/twiga-docs/"><i class="bi bi-book"></i> Docs</a>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="project-item">
  <div class="project-header">
    <span class="project-name">MLPForecast</span>
    <span class="project-year">2023</span>
  </div>
  <p class="project-desc">MLP-based net-load forecasting with uncertainty quantification for low-voltage distribution networks. Companion code for the published IEEE TPWRS paper.</p>
  <div class="project-links">
    <a href="https://github.com/sambaiga/mlpforecast"><i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub</a>
    <a href="https://doi.org/10.1109/TPWRS.2024.3400123"><i class="bi bi-journal-text"></i> Paper</a>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="project-item">
  <div class="project-header">
    <span class="project-name">MLCFCD</span>
    <span class="project-year">2023</span>
  </div>
  <p class="project-desc">Conformal forecasting with probabilistic predictions and coverage guarantees for distribution networks with photovoltaic generation.</p>
  <div class="project-links">
    <a href="https://github.com/sambaiga/MLCFCD"><i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub</a>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="project-item">
  <div class="project-header">
    <span class="project-name">Deep-NILMtk</span>
    <span class="project-year">2020</span>
  </div>
  <p class="project-desc">Community deep learning toolkit for non-intrusive load monitoring with modular pipelines, automation, and hyperparameter management.</p>
  <div class="project-links">
    <a href="https://github.com/BHafsa/Deep-NILMtk"><i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub</a>
    <a href="https://doi.org/10.1109/TII.2022.3206322"><i class="bi bi-journal-text"></i> Paper</a>
  </div>
</div>

</div>

## Selected Publications

<div class="pub-list">

<div class="pub-item">
  <p class="pub-title"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1109/TPWRS.2024.3400123">Efficiency through Simplicity: MLP-based Approach for Net-Load Forecasting with Uncertainty Estimates in Low-Voltage Distribution Networks</a></p>
  <p class="pub-meta">IEEE Transactions on Power Systems, Vol. 40, No. 1, pp. 46–57 · 2025</p>
</div>

<div class="pub-item">
  <p class="pub-title"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1109/TII.2022.3206322">Unlocking the Full Potential of Neural NILM: On Automation, Hyperparameters and Modular Pipelines</a></p>
  <p class="pub-meta">IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics, Vol. 19, No. 5, pp. 7002–7010 · 2023</p>
</div>

<div class="pub-item">
  <p class="pub-title"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1109/TSG.2020.3010621">Adaptive Weighted Recurrence Graphs for Appliance Recognition in Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring</a></p>
  <p class="pub-meta">IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid, Vol. 12, No. 1, pp. 398–406 · 2021</p>
</div>

<div class="pub-item">
  <p class="pub-title"><a href="https://doi.org/10.1109/TSG.2020.3010621">FPSeq2Q: Fully Parameterized Sequence to Quantile Regression for Net-Load Forecasting with Uncertainty Estimates</a></p>
  <p class="pub-meta">IEEE Transactions on Smart Grid, Vol. 13, No. 3, pp. 2440–2451 · 2022</p>
</div>

</div>

<p style="margin-top:1rem;font-size:0.875rem;color:#64748B;">Full publication list on <a href="https://sambaiga.github.io/pages/publication/index.html">sambaiga.github.io</a> and <a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0002-7838-533X">ORCID</a>.</p>
